title: Time-Ordered CatBoost Instead of Plain Category Handling
summary: Current strong runs use category dtypes and frequency encoding, but not a model that natively builds leakage-resistant categorical statistics in chronological order.
rationale: Current strong runs use category dtypes and frequency encoding, but not a model that natively builds leakage-resistant categorical statistics in chronological order. CatBoost’s ordered boosting plus `has_time` is a strong adjacent-competition pattern when high-cardinality IDs matter and row order is meaningful.
implementation_hint: Sort by `Year,Race,Driver,LapNumber`, keep raw categorical columns, and test CatBoost with `has_time=True`, `boosting_type="Ordered"`, plus the existing numeric features. Compare against the current GBDT baseline under race-grouped CV and watch whether unseen 2025 drivers/races improve.
expected_effect: Could add a small but real gain by extracting richer Driver/Race/Compound interactions without manual target encoding leakage.
risk: If the row order is not reconstructed correctly, quality can get worse; CatBoost may also be slower and harder to tune than the current baseline.
